zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Anne Moodye於1530年出生在英國素福爾可時，她的父親Edmund Moody32歲，母親Ursula Anne Sadlar28歲。她和Roger Wilson至少有5個兒子和7個女兒。她去世於1620年在英國越可爾丹別 維斯可享年90歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可莫爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Moodye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Moody  .

variants.name: Moody
meanings: English and Irish: nickname from Middle English mody ‘proud, haughty, angry, fierce, bold, brave, or rash’ (Old English mōdig). Compare Muddiman . This English name has been established in Ireland since the late 13th century.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Anne Moodye was born in 1530, in Suffolk, England, her father, Edmund Moody, was 32 and her mother, Ursula Anne Sadlar, was 28. She had at least 5 sons and 7 daughters with Roger Wilson. She died in 1620, in Danby Wiske, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 90, and was buried in Moulton, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Anne Moodye est née en 1530, à Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Moody, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Ursula Anne Sadlar, avait 28 ans. Elle a eu au moins 5 fils et 7 filles avec Roger Wilson. Elle est décédée en 1620, à Danby Wiske, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 90 ans, et a été enterrée à Moulton, Suffolk, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Anne Moodye(이)가 1530년 영국 수폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund Moody(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ursula Anne Sadlar(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 Roger Wilson(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1620년 영국 요르크실르 단아비 와이스크에서 90세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 모우턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Anne Moodye родилась в 1530 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund Moody было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Ursula Anne Sadlar, 28. У неё было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 7 дочерей от Roger Wilson. Она умерла в 1620 г. в местности данби виск, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 90 лет и была похоронена в местности моултон, суффолк, Англия.
